paper_title: Velocity Structure in Marine Sediments with Gas Hydrate Reflectors in Offshore SW Taiwan, from OBS Data Tomography

methods.description: This method involves performing 2-D tomographic modeling using the Tomo2D software to obtain an initial image of the velocity structure along the profiles. It uses a hybrid method for forward modeling and regularized LSQR solution for inversion to derive a reliable initial velocity model for 2-D inversion.

steps.description: Perform 2-D tomographic modeling using Tomo2D to obtain an initial image of the velocity structure.
steps.searchable_summary: use Tomo2D for 2-D tomographic modeling to image velocity structure

steps.description: Use a hybrid method based on graph and ray-bending methods for forward solution.
steps.searchable_summary: apply hybrid graph and ray-bending methods for forward solution

steps.description: Perform inversion using regularized LSQR solution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform inversion with regularized LSQR solution

steps.description: Construct an initial 1-D velocity model modified from previous studies.
steps.searchable_summary: construct initial 1-D velocity model from previous studies

steps.description: Generate primary refractions, reflections, and corresponding seismograms using RAYAMP-PC.
steps.searchable_summary: use RAYAMP-PC to generate refractions, reflections, and seismograms

steps.description: Adjust interfaces and velocity contrasts until satisfactory matches between observed and synthetic record sections are achieved.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust model interfaces and velocity contrasts for match with observations

steps.description: Convert the model to a grid model for Tomo2D.
steps.searchable_summary: convert velocity model to grid model for Tomo2D

steps.description: Evaluate improvements to the velocity model using root-mean-square and normalized chi squared statistical tests.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate model improvements with statistical tests

steps.description: Investigate solution quality using a checkerboard test procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: perform checkerboard test for solution quality

methods.description: This method describes the acquisition of seismic data along four transects in SW Taiwan using ocean bottom seismometers (OBS) and multi-channel seismic (MCS) data.

steps.description: Acquire seismic refraction data using 6 French IFEMER's ocean bottom seismometers.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire seismic refraction data with ocean bottom seismometers

steps.description: Apply a 4-33 Hz band-pass filtering to the OBS data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply band-pass filtering to OBS data

steps.description: Fire an air-gun at a 30-s interval at about 4 knots to yield spaced seismic traces of 50 m.
steps.searchable_summary: fire air-gun for seismic trace acquisition

steps.description: Correlate main horizons such as direct water arrivals and reflections between seafloor and BSR seen in the MCS section to the OBS-recordings.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate MCS horizons with OBS recordings

steps.description: Use only the first arrivals picked from offsets up to 3 km in the seismic inversion.
steps.searchable_summary: select first arrivals for seismic inversion
